references.name: mindmap_display_layout
references.description: Mermaid mindmap uses radial auto-layout. Default depth 3 for all top-level groups. Architecture and constraints omitted in normal mode. Session has near_memory and far_memory at level 1; near_memory at depth 4 (shows category children); near_memory children are top-level group names (conversation, conventions, work, documentation) with recent activities under each category; always show all 4 categories even when empty (display '(none)'). Deep subtrees shrink the radial layout — keep balanced by controlling depth. Full mode: all nodes expanded at max depth. User can request specific branches at specific depths. Section 3: memory stats table (Store/Count/Size/Tokens/Loaded) showing disk vs context occupancy per store, with /context hint.

references.name: theme_default
references.description: Use mermaid default theme for mindmap rendering. Always include %%{init: {'theme': 'default', 'mindmap': {'useMaxWidth': true}}}%% directive in output. This provides automatic multi-color nodes per branch depth and forces full available width. Custom blue theme is not supported by mermaid mindmap type.

references.name: far_memory_topic_splitting
references.description: When far_memory.json grows large, Claude splits it into topic-based archive files. Each archive groups messages by summarized subject (from near_memory summaries). Main far_memory.json keeps only the active conversation topic. Archive files are stored in sessions/archives/ named by topic slug (e.g., far_memory_architecture_design.json). Main file maintains an 'archives' index array with {file, topic, message_range, near_memory_range}. On split, Claude identifies topic boundaries from near_memory summary clusters, extracts completed topics to archive files, and updates the index. mind-context default view shows the last active conversation from near_memory.

references.name: programs_over_improvisation
references.description: Claude-as-engine is bootstrap only (new session, resume, compaction recovery). All mechanical operations use Python scripts in scripts/. Claude provides intelligence (summaries, topic names) as arguments to deterministic programs. When architecture evolves, scripts must be updated to match — programs ARE the implementation of the architecture.

references.name: staging_paths
references.description: Two staging paths from work. Convention staging: when a pattern proves reusable, extract it to conventions.json as a self-contained template — conventions never reference far_memory, they must be reproducible standalone by any future session. Content staging: documents, code, artifacts stay linked to memory — work.json points to far_memory ranges so the full creation context can be recalled for continuation or revision.

references.name: node_text_rules
references.description: Mindmap node text must be human-readable descriptions only. No raw IDs, internal pointers, or inline refs in node labels. Avoid mermaid reserved words (e.g. 'mindmap', 'graph', 'flowchart') in node text as they cause syntax errors.
